title: lupinus pilosus subsp. tassilicus
intro: Lupinus pilosus subsp. tassilicus, also known as the Atlas lupine, is a perennial plant species in the legume family native to the Atlas Mountains in North Africa. The Atlas lupine has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. It is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, due to its rich content of isoflavones and other bioactive compounds. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or decoction, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
